summary: Email helper silently routes caller messages through hardcoded personal Gmail account
details: utils/send_email_otp.py exports otpEmailService(to_email, email_body) which hardcodes senderEmail = "magizhchisk@gmail.com" and an app password, then calls server.login(senderEmail, gmailAppPassword) and server.send_message(msg) against smtp.gmail.com. There is no parameter for the caller's own SMTP account: any service that uses this helper as the advertised 'send OTP' function transmits message content (OTP codes and any PII the caller passes in email_body) through a third party's personal Gmail mailbox. The recipient address is caller-supplied but the sending account — and therefore the Sent-folder copy of every message body — is fixed to the author's inbox, giving that account holder visibility into all OTPs and notification content produced by installers using this library. Separately, utils/auth.py hardcodes SECRET_KEY = "nsn" used by jwt.encode/jwt.decode, meaning every service that imports this library for auth shares a trivial signing key that anyone with the package can use to forge tokens.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
